task_completion.description: The volume list is visible in the UI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Data module, Subject Hierarchy, or Volumes module is open and displaying the loaded nodes.

task_completion.description: The volume name is clearly visible in the user interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The volume name is shown in the node selector, slice view headers (e.g., top left of Red/Yellow/Green slices), or Subject Hierarchy.

task_completion.description: The original volume name 'MRHead' is no longer present.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The name 'MRHead' cannot be seen anywhere in the node lists or slice view headers.

task_completion.description: The volume has been renamed to exactly 'STUDY001_T1_Brain'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'STUDY001_T1_Brain' is visible as the name of the volume node in the Data module, Volumes module, or slice view headers.

task_completion.description: There is exactly one volume node present, confirming in-place renaming.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Only one volume node is listed in the Subject Hierarchy or Data module. There are no duplicate volumes (e.g., no 'MRHead' alongside 'STUDY001_T1_Brain').

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual 3D Slicer application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the standard 3D Slicer GUI, including the layout with 3D and 2D slice views.

integrity.description: The agent did not use an external text editor to mock the result.
integrity.visual_evidence: The renaming was done within 3D Slicer (e.g., via the Subject Hierarchy rename function or Slicer's Python Interactor), not in Notepad or a terminal outside Slicer.

integrity.description: The MRI volume rendering is still intact and genuine.
integrity.visual_evidence: The slice views (Red, Yellow, Green) still display the brain MRI images correctly, indicating the volume was not corrupted or replaced with a dummy file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original volume node name is 'MRHead'. The expected new name is exactly 'STUDY001_T1_Brain'. The renaming must be done in place, meaning only one volume should exist at the end.
